title: Brain Protocol v2 record envelope
description: The common envelope every record carries on the wire, per BP-01 §5. The four primitives are frozen: entity, activity, edge, action. type: 'goal' and type: 'attribute' are invalid by construction (BP-01 §3). Absent optional fields are omitted, never null; unknown fields are ignored for validation and passed through opaquely (BP-01 §14). Subtype base terms are enumerated in vocabulary.json; unknown subtypes received without a mapping pass through opaquely and are therefore not closed-enum here (BP-01 §10-§11).

id.description: Canonical wire identifier: urn:brain:<system>:<rtype>:<uuid> (BP-01 §4). Minted by the emitting system, stable for the record's life. The rtype segment must equal the record's type (enforced per type branch below).

type.description: The primitive discriminator. Exactly four values; a fifth primitive is a MAJOR version event (BP-01 §3.1).

subtype.description: The wire name for what v0.1 called kind/activity_type/predicate/action_type. Base terms: see vocabulary.json (entity_subtypes, activity_subtypes, edge_predicates). Action verbs are named by the capability they invoke and validated against the grant matrix (BP-03), not the vocabulary. Unmapped unknown terms pass through opaquely (BP-01 §11).

source.description: Which system asserted this record; also a method value (manual, derived, agent-inference) where applicable (BP-01 §5.2).

external_ref.description: The source's own stable name for the record; the key for idempotent sync, entity resolution and forget. MUST except on edges (BP-01 §5.2).

provenance.description: Record URNs this record was derived from (record-level derivation). MUST on derived or inferred records; a derived record without provenance is a forget leak and non-conformant (BP-01 §5.2).

origin_chain.description: Ordered system ids the record has passed through (system-level lineage; the BP-04 loop guard). MUST on synced/re-emitted records (BP-01 §5.2).

owner.description: Opaque member reference (a local member id or a person-entity URN): whose life the record belongs to. Anchors the visibility law (BP-02) and the member lens (BP-03).

actor.description: Who or what created/performed the record. Owner and actor are different facts. On actions this is proposed_by (BP-01 §5.3, §5.7).

subject.description: What the record is about. MUST on edges; OPTIONAL on activities (a denormalised convenience; the edge remains the source of truth).

object.description: The target of the edge's predicate. MUST on edges only.

valid_time.description: When the fact happened or became true. RFC 3339 UTC with the Z designator. MUST on every record, every class (CD-5). When interval is present, valid_time MUST equal interval.start (BP-01 §6).

system_time.description: When the asserting system learned it. RFC 3339 UTC. MUST on every record, every class (CD-5). The degenerate form valid_time == system_time is permitted where the two genuinely coincide.

interval.description: The single wire spelling for every span. An open-ended span omits end (never null). A point-in-time record omits interval entirely (BP-01 §6).

state.description: Where the record is in its type's state machine (BP-01 §7); partitioned values in vocabulary.json. MUST on activities and actions; OPTIONAL on entities; not used on edges.

visibility.description: Who may see the record: private | shared:<scope> | public. Registered scopes: partners, adults, household (vocabulary.json). Unknown scope ⇒ receiver treats as private (CD-6). public never means the open internet (BP-01 §8).

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity class (BP-07); starter classification by subtype in vocabulary.json. Stamped by the asserting system; receivers MAY upgrade, MUST NOT downgrade. Note: an S3 payload never travels on any automatic protocol path — reference pointers travel at their own (≤ S2) class (BP-07 §2.4).

confidence.description: How sure the asserting system is. Absent means asserted-as-fact by the source; emitters MUST NOT use confidence: 1.0 to mean fact — they omit the field. MUST on merge proposals and on derived/inferred records (BP-01 §5.5).

attributes.description: Subtype-specific fields. attributes.name is MUST on entities; attributes.title is MUST on activities; attributes.summary SHOULD be present. Money is integer minor units plus ISO 4217 currency (amount_minor, currency). All-day flag travels as attributes.all_day; recurrence as attributes.recurrence (RFC 5545 RRULE). A venue-anchored scheduled event carries attributes.tz as an IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/London) so a consumer renders a stable wall-clock time across DST; valid_time stays a UTC instant for ordering (BP-01 §5.5, §6).

allOf.$comment: Entity profile (BP-01 §5.6): external_ref, owner, attributes.name required; id rtype = entity; state, if present, from the entity/goal machine.

Activity profile (BP-01 §5.6, §7): external_ref, owner, state, attributes.title required; id rtype = activity. The state union covers the activity machine and the goal machine (a goal may be emitted as an activity, BP-01 §3.2).

Edge profile (BP-01 §5.6): subject and object required; no state machine — edge lifecycle is its validity interval; id rtype = edge. An edge's visibility and sensitivity must each be at least as strict as the stricter of its two endpoints (not machine-checkable from a single record).

Action profile (BP-01 §5.6, §5.7): the Action extension — summary, payload, requires_confirm — plus external_ref and state; id rtype = action. Full action constraints (gates, needs_human) in action.schema.json.
The reserved subtype goal (BP-01 §3.2, CD-9): emitted as entity or activity only, never a fifth primitive; target, measure and horizon are required attributes.
